claim_text: Patient reports right shoulder pain.
Patient injured his right shoulder after being tackled during a football game.
Pain experienced began about four days ago.
Patient had an appendectomy three to four years ago.
Patient denies fever.
Patient denies neck pain.
Patient denies elbow or wrist pain.
Patient is taking Tylenol.
Blood pressure is 122/76.
Heart rate is 82 bpm.
Oxygenation is 95% on room air.
No tenderness to palpation of the cervical spine.
Full range of motion on cervical exam.
No ecchymosis or bruising on the right shoulder.
Pain to palpation at the acromioclavicular joint.
Decreased abduction and adduction to 90 degrees.
Pain with supination and pronation of the right arm.
Palpable radial pulse.
Right shoulder x-ray showed acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Patient has right shoulder acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Patient will use a sling for right shoulder.
Patient will take Meloxicam 15mg once a day.
Patient will have a cat scan of the right shoulder.
Patient will be referred to physical therapy to strengthen right shoulder.
Patient will need to avoid tackle football for a while.
Patient will not participate in active contact sports until healing occurs.
